But As a matter of fact remarkably some people were actually offended at the suggestion I Say remarkably because they'd all been indicted so see it seemed to me sort of unreasonable to be offended but nonetheless all right about Hopefully about 30 minutes of preliminary housekeeping nonsense so and then we can Can get to work so you should have on page to a page that says rules of the road and the rules of the road are The following first of all No electronic duties Especially any that make noise So if you're a bail bondsman or a cardiac surgeon And somebody may have to reach you You could have one but put it on vibrate and The rest of you don't get caught with one because it's a hundred dollar fine, and I'm not kidding Let's see who's the biggest most scariest guy Yeah, where's Zach? There you are stand up Zach Stand up Hey, okay now you won't be able to understand them, but okay, but but cuz you know He's from somewhere that You know, I don't know what they speak but kangaroo or something but anyway But he's a but he's a big guy. Okay, and so he's my enforcer so Zach anybody's phone I pat thing bark sings rings it's a hundred bucks to my favorite charity, okay, which is me Charity begins at home So no gizmo's none none none none none none Okay Comfort okay, nobody wants to hear about room too warm room too cold Can't see okay do what you need to do Okay Ladies you can take everything off if you want guys you can get down to your skivvies if you're too warm if you're cold Borrow something buy something move away from a vent if you can't see come sit up here on the floor Do whatever you got to do but no whining Okay, nor does Denise want to hear about they're not being soap in your room or the window not open on or the toilet Not flush in the front desk is that way okay Brakes we're going to be under enormous time pressure Because you all got seven million questions, which I'm going to try and accommodate and And I have a lot of stuff for you more than we can possibly do so You need to be we give it time to be back from a break you need to be not milling around and have to be rounded up And heard it in but like back Hey Q&A by the way the way that works is Periodically we will have some question-answer time We will have more tomorrow than we have today, but we will have some question-answer time and at that time You can scurry up to that table and grab a seat Okay, and then you get to play Q&A and When you're done you leave and somebody else can have a seat for as long as it goes so you get to sit down and I get to sit And you don't have to stand up at an aisle or yell or whatever. Okay, so that's the way Q&A work Really really really really good questions Two or three a day Get a gift get a prize Really really really bad questions You may not get to ask another question for the entire time that you're here Let's see being offended hopefully you will be At some point I sort of pride myself on it I First of all have this theory that if you haven't offended somebody by noon every day, you're not you know, you're not doing much And Generally, that's like one-on-one. So when there's a group it happens more frequently So I may offend you because of some gender remark I may offend you because of some ethnicity remark I may offend you because of some money remark. I may offend you because I swear Who knows but probably every single one of you will be offended multiple times during the course of the two and a half days together You can decide whether to let that get in the way of your mission or not. I Suggest to you there's no profit in it It's not going to put any money in your pocket. It's not going to help you in your copywriting business to be offended So the smarter thing is to ignore it and focus on what you can get that is useful to you It's very hard to edit In this kind of environment Some people are better at it than others. I'm terrible at it. So as time goes on I kind of relax up here and with you and I don't have my Internal editor on stopping me from saying something before I say it So mostly it's not that I'm trying to offend you actually I don't care, but it's not that I'm trying but just happens but You can choose how you respond We do there will be not often and not many But there will be some resources presented to you during the course of the two days Nobody's holding a gun against your head. So you can keep your hand firmly clamped on your wallet if you want It too shall pass But there are Some resources that as I prepared the seminar. I thought had to be mentioned And so they will be we also have three sponsors. You've probably met them by now I'll speak to them in a second But but they are picking up the tab for all the grub Which is nice But more importantly they too were chosen because I thought they had really really relevant things That could be helpful to you. You will hear from two of them during your lunches Although you are welcome to go somewhere else for lunch if you would like But you will hear from one each during lunch and you will hear from one first thing tomorrow morning We are not going to get through everything in your man first of all It would be better if you didn't get way ahead of me So like if you stayed up till three o'clock in the morning reading it that probably didn't help And I wouldn't recommend you do it tonight. Although by tonight you probably won't be staying up to read anything, but There's no way we're going to get through everything so I Will skip over some stuff as we go You cannot let that drive you nuts Just get what we can get Done get what you can get and don't obsess over what you're not getting However, you will leave with unanswered questions. So I There's a mechanism I have for trying to accommodate that actually two mechanisms One there will be a follow-up tell a seminar I Don't have a date for you yet, but there will be a follow-up group called That you can all come to when we will do question and answer secondly, I will gang them and Collect them from you and Do some sort of? Memo because many of them will be the same But I will try and answer many of your unanswered questions in one big written document that goes to everybody You have until May 21st to get those into a WAI So as you accumulate your unanswered questions when you get home and you have a week or so to ponder You can put them in writing you can get them to a WAI and I'll clump them all together and I will answer them in some sort of written memo that everybody gets On some page you should have an agenda It might be page three Yes, yes, okay We're gonna stay as close to it as we can the end of today might be might go a little longer We might go to six before we break their mind backing everything up But we're gonna stick to it as close as we can Tonight you have a dinner break on your own lunch is both both days lunch will be buffet outside the room You get your grub you get back in here And that's what the round tables are for So at least some of you can not sit at your desks if you would like and There's a presentation both days at lunch the second half hour of the break and then you have 15 minutes afterwards for bathroom cigarette break who smokes Everybody smoke Well good for this group however you have 15 minutes after their presentation of lunch every day to run outside and smoke Tonight there are bonus sessions that means they are optional You will not hurt my feelings. So if the topic is not of tremendous interest to you Feel free to go get some rest or go watch the basketball game or do whatever it is that you would like to do so The bonus sessions in order are one about information marketing and I will make brief remarks and then do Q&A so that is really how to be your own client and publish for your own profit There will be a wealth attraction What we think about money question and answer session after that and there will be a question and answer session about being a published author of books after that Again attendance is not required So you have an agenda that we are going to try and stick to Saturday night Saturday night On the whenever we take a break this morning I'm going to give Denise this stack of little goodies You all anybody who is going Saturday night will need to get one of these big green ones which is your admission pass and Your and your ticket for food If you lose them you're paying to get in and you're paying for your food So don't lose your pass if you are driving rather than riding the bus You also need a parking coupon if you would like to park for free So you can get one of those from Denise also, okay? Maybe smarter than you. They're very smart direct response pros and so Not only are they the source of stuff, but they can be very very helpful to you With the theme idea with what will work for your client what will work within a given budget they do both B2B and consumer and The other second thing you should know about them is they're really good at sourcing this stuff and are often Same price or less Then some direct vendors we used to use in the past including going overseas to get this stuff and having to buy it Much bigger quantities and so forth. So they're really good at it. So you should think of them really as your back office for Object mail dimensional mail these kinds of creative direct mail campaigns. They're gonna read it out of obligation So so you want to visit with with Travis? They're good back office resource. He's going to show you some great examples during his launch presentation and I think I think they also probably have some kind of tip that you can like fake home with you And pull ideas from like boozy at copy doodles Mike has become sort of the copy cosmetics guy in my world And is it is my contention by the way that The look is often as important as the copy and It is routine for copywriters to end off good copy and then have the graphics people kill it And so I never give up control over personally And I don't suggest that any copywriter does I consider graphic arts people probably some of the room. I consider them are mortal enemies Unless they are unless they are managed most carefully so copy doodles really Sort of automates the whole process of Junking up direct mail Marking up direct mail illustrating direct mail at or print or online media and academy adding cartoons to it So a Mike really has like mastered this and has developed a great Software product and an online product that really makes it a snap to do it well Two things about that for you one. It's a great way to bring added value to the table with a client Because you can easily deliver something more than raw copy Secondly it's easy it makes it easier for you to stay in control Of what this thing is supposed to look like And third again, it's a beat the control tool Because often You could be to control by changing the look Rather than changing the copy and the more the the more sophisticated a client you work with The harder it is to be a control because they either knew what they were doing or they hired somebody who knew what they were doing And so personally often The clients I'm dealing with are already using smart people so Copy is often the least place I get to go to actually have an impact on what they've done But some of these other things could have an impact on what they've done So you'll enjoy Mike. He's good speaker. He's got great stuff for you. So he'll be with you tomorrow morning Dean killing back for you probably that there's Dean Dean his company get customers now Reliently has assembled Turnkey done direct mail campaigns Both for new customer acquisition for a variety of kinds of businesses particularly the small local business Operating on a local level but not exclusively and especially in the area of new resident new mover and Whether new customer acquisition or bring customer back birthday campaigns that consistently delivered double-digit response percentages into the 20s and the 30s And and are almost as reliable as the sun rising setting he also is Has figured out the whole access these lists Effectively gained so he's a real resource in terms of the mailing lists as well He too you can think of as a back office resource for you Whether modifying his campaigns or literally Integrating them in a system you're delivering to a client and using them as is So Dean gets Exceptional results with this direct mail there's a lot to be learned from looking at it and again He's a good back office resource as well. So those are Your sponsors and your speakers We are going to talk about Client management before we talk about client attraction That will seem Odd too many of you and it probably is not what any of you would like Not only will I offend everybody by the way, but I'll probably disappoint everybody before We we get through with this too So you would probably want to talk about getting them first And you would probably want to spend the lion's share of the time on getting them We are actually going to do night. We are going to talk about getting them after we talk about managing them And we are probably going to spend a tick more time on managing them than we are on getting them The reason for that is possibly contrary to your experience to date Getting them is not the biggest problem of the challenges of our business And I promise you by the time we get done You'll have options to go get them that aren't particularly difficult and it doesn't matter whether So we have a wide variety of folks in the room. So it doesn't matter whether you're beginner or whether you're a I think we have a couple people in the high six figs. There's a list in your manual of who's here not by name, but By money and so That doesn't matter because process is largely the same but the attraction part which we'll get to this afternoon Has a how you think component that we're going to have to talk about and then it has a mechanical Here's where to find him and here's what to do to get him component If I if you build it You would have us talking about the mechanical Where the hell are they and how can I get them thing first and most? It's actually going to be the third thing we talk about and it's the thing. We're going to hurry the most but because it is Mechanic And it is not as hard as perhaps your experience has taught you that it is it does not require as much time as Managing them once you get them now the other reason To focus on management so just make the assumption this morning that you have no trouble getting clients There's plenty of them to get at whatever level it is that you're at that you want them So just live with that assumption for the morning But understand that steel Getting them is the thing most copywriters like doing the least Most people become writers For the same reason people become dentists they have really crappy personalities and They and they don't really like people They hate the thought of selling except that people come to copywriting selling they have a profound advantage But and they would really prefer to be under a tree with a laptop left the hell alone writing Not out running around getting clients so and secondly What mom-and-pop shop approach to this business tends to create the The the roller coaster effect of Get project now lock self-in-room for two months to work on project finish project and realize you're unemployed And now try and fix that again So because of those two realities What we do with a client when we get them is critically important because a we want to keep them Be we want to get more work from them. So we don't have to go find another one And we want to expand the range and scope and size of everything we do with them and can do with them So we get a lot of money from the ones that we have and are in this endless run around Anx of where am I going to get the next one? Where am I going to get the next one? Where am I going to get the next one? Etc etc so the management thing is really important. So we're going to talk about that first Attraction second do not let that stir If you look at page six You will find The three Issues of this business So client attraction and management is one What's that? What is that Cute a one pass one Under bucks next time I Hate gadgets. Oh by the way if I catch any of you guys Talking on a cell phone while you're peeing that's a hundred bucks to Well women may not know This is the moron behavior of the day So by the way if your husband or your boyfriend is calling you and you hear that it ain't a fountain Hey, he's actually he's actually talking to you while he's peeing This is this is the male idea of multi-tasking I'm just going to whack you right over the head take the damn thing out of your hand and flush it and charge you a hundred bucks for being stupid so Watch for me when I come in the men's room so client attraction and management The actual efficacy Can you write? Can you deliver writing that works? We're not going to talk a lot about that, but we are going to talk about the strategy that makes it possible to do well with a client and compensation getting paid Those are the three aspects of the game and we will spend a Lot of time on client attraction and management and compensation and a little bit on strategy so But I skip those notes and let you go to the money. Take a look at page eight. So page eight is Really brilliantly simple and so everybody like gets it, but not everybody Behaves as if they get it So if we use a number just for the sake of conversation of a million dollars a year Which I realized not everybody needs wants desires About a year, but let's just use that number The real question is how do we get to it and So you can get to it the McDonald's way so you get to every million a dollar at a time One cheeseburger at a time That's fine if you're McDonald's because you have 50,000 restaurants So like they all sell a cheeseburger every hour You're kicking butt taking names But that's not where you and I live So Here's what's important to plug into your head The smaller the clients capability To use Smaller the clients capability to pay you for coffee So if you think local small companies small business So right here we are halfway between Cleveland and Akron We have access to both markets as well as All of this small town market around us We could go find small business clients who could pay us a thousand bucks Over over the year to do a few things for them and if we do that How many of them do we have to have? To make our million We got to have a thousand of them That means we got to find roughly How many a month a really bad a hundred eighty five something like that we got to find two and a half every day It will be easier Easier to find a hundred clients who could pay us ten thousand Then it will be to find a thousand pay us a thousand And there are plenty of small businesses and small companies who can pay us ten thousand each Now not they don't think they can The first moment that we talked to So if you're premise With the guy that owns this hotel is you're gonna pay me ten thousand dollars this year And I'm gonna write copy for your ads in marketing That's not gonna be a good premise because he doesn't think you can do that But we know we can do that because we can increase his business by a hundred thousand two hundred thousand three hundred thousand dollars And therefore ten thousand bucks is not So it is easier to get a hundred of them than a thousand of the little ones Also, if you get in the habit of getting the really little ones It clogs up your whole system so you can't get and deal with a bigger one So the first thing to get out of your head is it's not really harder to get back And The very fact that we only have to find a hundred well now we got it down to maybe a manageable number If we bring it down to 50, that's an even more Manageable number if we get down to where we only need say 20 clients in a year In all probability, we're gonna get most of that by referrals All right, our endless chain of referrals can take care of that if we need 200 We're gonna need 50 different ways to get them. So these are things you need to think about When you think about how you stock the pond of your business you need to think about the monetary worth of each client And how many of them you need to meet your particular Income goal and I would say to you the fewer the better Then your range of capability the fewer the better so if you reduce the number of units you need Your task is going to get easy page 9 Shows you for things About Making money from right so there is the thing you all know very well Right actually the top two are probably very familiar to you And things you want to do So one is writing copy for their advertising their marketing materials their websites their brochures and so The second category is what I would call non marketing right All the other stuff that a business can use that really is not so Response measurable Then the bottom two Which we have a little bonus session about tonight are important for two reasons One because they make client attraction a lot easier And they make client attraction a lot easier in many ways One because they establish you as something more than a vendor and a copy and to They create income Or They at least create offset For the expense of promoting and marketing yourself in order to attract clients so If I go speak for example To let's take a really bad gig but where we still might get a client So I have to go back a ways because I don't do these bad things anymore, but I used to do a lot of them So let's say I go do the well here's a bad one. I remember so I go do the Well I can give you one more current because I actually did a current one as a favor So I go do the Cleveland sales and marketing executives club after dinner So this is bad for a number of reasons one the sales and marketing executives club There's really hardly anybody there who has anything to do with sales or marketing There's a lot of secretaries and there's a lot of managers and that's a lot of business owners, which is the only good thing Secondly, it's after dinner, which also means it's after a bar This is always a bad environment to be speaking at least it's a cash bar not an open bar, but still So there's going to be at least straighter people there There's going to be 30 of them south of their minds and there's going to be somebody who's going to be different Third there's no fee so that's unhappy because I get paid So I can go do that gig and Hope I get a client Or I can go do that gig and Even in the bad environment and even if I do it gently and even if I'm no good at it 300 people there. There's 30 that'll be south, but there's also 30 Who are? Searcher seekers who want to do better So 15 of them will buy books cds some sort of package of mine Even if I'm no good at presenting it There's 15 of them who will buy just because they buy everything And they're also typically the most successful progressive folks in the room. It's not a coincidental relationship, by the way So I can go pick up 3 000 bucks for being there Or I can go pick up nothing. The difference is whether I have stuff or I don't Have stuff now my likelihood of getting a client goes up not down if I have stuff right a because it raises me above a vendor to them and the 15 people who buy the stuff are the most likely client prospects and I have a Permission to follow up with them without looking like I'm pursuing them because they gave me money and got stuff So I can put a tail on it Of a tele seminar. I can put a tail on it Local if I want to follow up mastermind meeting for free Over lunch at the pancake house. I can do all this sort of overt stuff without it looking overt because they paid money for this Package of stuff right if I want to I can now I got three thousand dollars to spend that I didn't have before I kind of know I'm going to have it beforehand so I could spend it ahead of time So I could send one of my books to everybody who's coming to prep them So now people are walking in the door Coming up wanting me to autograph a book They have read some stuff. They're better prepared for me than they would have been otherwise Um, I can give them all goody bags when they get there which puts them in a good mood Or I can spend the three thousand bucks on the backside Do and follow up, but I'm doing it with their three thousand instead of my three thousand So these two bottom things are extremely important And they are points of differentiation for you So they ought really be part of your business The last piece of good news about this is when you write for yourself, of course The client is pretty much always satisfied Um, um, I gave you a list of kind of who's here. We're not going to spend time on it But if out of curiosity you want to see it. It's there um the list of Everybody's books that they listed is there I bold faced the ones that actually have something to do With your business And left the others Uh, not involved faced I would say that From the list of people everybody would pay money make a donation to go have lunch with And a list of books here's the one sort of criticism I would give for you to think about Is my deduction is you are not totally Um Irretrievably necessarily immersed in this business And at least for some period of time if you are climbing your way up to the income levels You kind of need to be So you sort of have to give up for example watching television for entertainment You have to be entertained by watching things on tv that are actually useful to you in one way shape or form Either business knowledge or specific Copywriting now so if you have a choice between dancing with the stars And an infomercial Unless you're doing work for a dance studio You would be better served watching the infomercial And take a note If you have a choice between watching dancing with the stars and jill rivers how they get so rich You ought to choose how they get so rich same with the books same with the people you would take the lunch or go to lunch with observation There's a list of things people were most surprised about when they got into freelancing So one is carol park says I didn't need 20 clients at a time to earn at these decent living Um, no, you don't some people need 200 at a time by the way Um, if they only charge them 75 bucks Um, and by the way, you will always find somebody out there who charges less than me Um, no matter how cheap you may be you will find somebody who will do this everybody's got a brother-in-law who thinks he can write Um And almost everybody's got an unemployed brother-in-law who thinks he can write Um, and the web stuff everybody's got a 12 year old Um, either in the house or next door uh, so You know you can you can you can be walmart if you want but uh, but no you don't need 20 clients On the other hand you don't want one Um, because one's a real deadly number in anything um Mormon guys even figured that out when it comes to the you know, but kidding um What the widely varied fees that clients are willing to pay for similar projects, where's my Well, you're right and you're wrong in that The fees clients will pay for something are all over the place um similar project maybe Similar handling of the client and the project by the Copywriter no, so the fee has to do with that right and so The bigger lesson Is how surprisingly elastic they are about what they'll pay If they'll handle it properly That's really the bigger lesson a lot of people underestimate the fee elasticity of a given client And it exists in selling everything We just don't think about the analogies So almost everybody goes to a car show room To buy a car now with a number in mind Even more so than a decade ago because they do all this prep because it's easy So they go online and they go to 50 websites and they go to the blue book Which you used to have to go find a blue book And now they have in their mind when they walk in that car show room a number 92 of all automobile buyers According to the america automobile sales association Spend 20 to 30 more than the number in their mind when they walk show So this exists in everything we buy So yeah, your client may have a number in mind, but if that doesn't matter it's an elastic number How bad a lot of copy is And yet how hard it is to find clients that's Cindy and then David very few business owners understand their customers and are willing to pay to improve their sales So both of those things are really the same right Right Well, there's here's Here's a very important Lesson about clients So i'm going to spend five minutes on it. I'm going to tell you a story first When i moved from here to phoenix in 1978 I hung out several shingles and one was speaking and i actually Had a line of educational cassette programs that weren't mine. So i was a distributor for somebody else's product And the only person in the world Ever to make six figures as a distributor of nightgill conan products if you know nightgill Is a guy by my name of joel welton Who lives at phoenix and so at the time joel was just getting started as a speaker and he was a nightgill conan So I was sort of going down the same path And I quickly became annoyed at joel because he had this stupid Like a soup can with a label on it. You'd see label success comes in cans not in canots And you know the ingredient panel four tablespoons a positive attitude this crap, right? Every office you went into this damn can is sitting there sometimes there's three or four right? So like after two weeks i'm just living So I go find them and take them to lunch And and so we talk about the business Joel told me something that I have applied in everything I've done since So here it is. He said when you walk in someone's office If you see hardly any books and you don't see this is before And you don't see any Audio cassette programs or you only see a couple of them get your ass out of there as fast as you can Drop a catalog on the desk say nice to meet you and get your ass out the door He said if you walk in the guy's office and there's crap piled everywhere There's books. There's cassette programs. There's tapes. You got to turn sideways to walk between stacks of them There's a ton of them with the shrink wrap still on them that he hasn't opened. There's product everywhere Stay as long as you can Spread your crap out as fast as you can and stay as long as you can Because a buyer is a buyer is a buyer is a buyer and converting virgins. It's hard difficult unpleasant expensive work If I went out to my car and I wrote it down Exactly the way you said He said it a little nicer by the way, but but I've since translated it to be more impactful So Here's the deal both David and Cindy's observation is astute and correct Contrary to logic the overwhelming percentage of business owners Small business and guys run a big company Our clueless and don't care And you are unlikely to be The person who turns them into clueless and motivated Other people better than you have been there before So the person who's doing really crappy advertising you think oh boy, I could fix this in my sleep Right. They're not your best prospect They're better than the person who's doing no advertising but barely Only in any field any population including this one Or all the hotel owners in Cleveland Or all the restaurant owners in Cleveland Or all the racetrack owners in america Or all the anything or just take the whole population this community doesn't make any difference You take a population and here's what you go fly 1% are kicking butt taking names. They got like all the money 4% got a little of it 15% are doing okay And 80% are clueless and broke Doesn't change and change nothing's changed it and it's not going to change All right The place to get the money is from the 5% not from the 95% You are far better off dealing with 5%ers than 95% So Yes, the vast majority of them are dumb They don't want to do anything aggressive to get customers They would rather sit in their restaurant gather cobwebs on them until it goes out of business Then do 20 smart things to fix that problem Your best client is somebody that's already doing 20 smart things because he wants to do 21 smart things And if you bring the 21st smart thing, you're going to find the welcome map So we want smart people not dumb people And we want people who are doing stuff that people are doing things. They're a thousand percent right So We have a fears list which we're going to get to those things I grouped them though for you so page 13 is a fears list. I just grouped them for you We're going to get to this later But the type a group these are all right off your questionnaires, by the way, these are verbatim So you can see You're not alone Type a These all have to do with self So these have nothing to do with anything but me Which is the overwhelming majority of them by the way the second group has to do with compensation So there's a lot of angst About money The third group is real practical stuff Leaving my corporate job with no salary and I got three kids and I got a dog and I got rent Running out of money That's a real practical issue. By the way, it's good to be afraid of that Because running out of money is a very bad thing Type d That I'll have to work all the time To pull it off My remark about that is probably So be sure so be sure you're doing stuff. You like doing Yeah because Yeah, you're going to work like all the time I mean it would not necessarily at a desk all the time But pretty much you're at least you'll be working up here All the time and if that's not something you want to do Six figures in this thing is probably out of reach And by the way, you got to get family and you know, you got to get them straightened out about all this So like here's early marriage conversation You're going to go downstairs and work on Christmas morning Well You aren't coming out of the bedroom until nine o'clock What difference does it make to anybody what I do from seven to nine o'clock? Why should you care? Right, you just make a lot of noise when you get up When my way back upstairs and enter the general population for the day But yeah in the morning I'm working And by the way if everybody that's over here gets real boring in the afternoon I'm going to disappear and go back to work for a little while Nobody will notice I'm gone. Don't worry about it. Okay And then the last one was kind of funny There's somebody who's worried about being a giant tax target for Obama. I chose not to name you but That's the most legitimate fear on the page On page 14, there's a list of stuff about me you're puzzled by I'm going to deal with those probably during the wealth session tonight. Some of them will go away. They'll become self-evident As we go along On page 17, you will find in every section of your manual. I have given you a page that looks like this To write down Your aha's by the way, if you have a really good one, you're allowed to stand up and yell it out Okay, and you might get a treat Okay But but but but the biggest thing you ought to know Is you ought not try and leave here with like 5 000 pages of notes Okay, that's utterly unproductive I see people in seminars now who are like trying to transcribe the seminar on their doohickey while they're sitting there Buy a book Okay, and stay home and save a ton of money That's not how you get value out of something like this You get value out of something like this not by what I say But by what you think of as a result of what I say And only a small percentage of what you think of is like good enough that you're going to do something with it So check it when you think of it and if now hey, this is something that's really write that down And if you get out of here with like 20 of those We've had a really good two days If you get out of here with 2 000 things we've had a really bad two days Because nothing stood out to you Is really really valuable and really really actionable So you just got a big pile of stuff and you're not going to do anything with it when you get home anyway So look for the big ones Don't try and get it all don't worry about getting it all if there's nine steps of this and you didn't quickly write them all Don't worry about that. That's not what we're here for We're here for change Not just mechanically, but internally get the big thing. So you got a page every so often to write them down Don't make this about bulk. Okay We're going to start some time client management. Let's go to page 21 So when it comes to client management, which is a polite term for client control Right, so you might want to make that substitution You got to totally end this idea that The client is in control of the relationship It's completely counterproductive. Right. It's certainly no good for you It's also no good for them If they should be in control of this they ought not have you They should be doing this Because this is the highest paid thing in their business. This is where all the money is is the marketing So if they are really capable of being in control of that They should be doing that They shouldn't be delegating that Okay, so They people should delegate to things the stuff. They're really crappy at Right or the stuff where there's somebody a lot better at it than they are That's what a smart business owner does He delegates two things stuff where he can find somebody who's really a lot better at it than he is Or something he's really horrid Right, but if he's better at it than everybody else he ought to do it. That's where the money is So you can't leave him in control. He shouldn't be in control. He'll mock the whole thing up So very quickly my weight watcher story So you may have heard it, but it's still You ought to hear it like a thousand times because then it'll set in So i'm doing a bunch of work for weight weight watchers corporate And weight watchers, I don't know they get bought and sold like every couple of years They were owned by Heinz when I was there And so the president of the moment which should have been the title on the door president of the moment um Very nice guy They brought him over from Italy. He was a Heinz brand manager of ketchup or something in Italy So they bring him over and they promote him and they make a president a weight watcher Um, which in and of itself is humorous So les is He's my height He's got a 28 inch waist which I had in junior high school. That's really what it means So I say great Plus as well i've been doing some calculations We're turns out we're paying you more per hour than we're paying me And i'm president of the company I wanted to say in your point, but I knew that wasn't going to be productive, you know, so I said okay I said less. Let me explain to you Why that's fine So do you know how to do everything here? Far better than I do you know this company inside and out Anything about running this company, you know except one thing How to get fat people to show up at weight watchers meetings and give you money About that you're clueless Unfortunately, everything else you know how to do Is valueless without that That's why I get paid more than you do, but we can keep it our little secret See at lunch out the door Never had the conversation again Now the point is That's truth He's a ceo And there's stuff presumably That he knows about running that company that I don't know But he does not know how to get fat people to come to weight watchers meetings. He hasn't got a client And the whole thing depends on getting fat people to come to weight watchers meetings. That's that that's where the money is Hey And so you can't let him be in control of that God forbid I sat in a meeting with them with their regular ad agency bat Barton Durson stupid and I actually saw him approve TV commercials They showed them proudly They got two stick figures stick figures Walking to the beach and sitting in little drawn Beach chairs talking to each other About bathing suit season stick figures First of all a kid could have done Right. I mean you could an etch a sketch for god's sakes. All right. Second of all We're selling stuff to fat people. You don't want to show them stick figures for god's sakes. Are you insane? Right He approved this campaign. You can't let him be in control of our stuff Hopeless right so not good for you not good for them So this is all about you Taking control So different attitudes probably than you used to so what are people afraid of so this is really all off your questionnaires So first of all People are afraid of charging too much. They're afraid of not being worth the money They're afraid of not being justified in getting the money They're afraid of not being qualified Somebody used this precise language afraid of being exposed as a fraud And then some smart person said i'm afraid of charging too little So let's let's let's talk about them all So charging too much and losing a client So the way most people respond to that is they under price and they negotiate And they back down when somebody challenges their feet That's the normal way to respond to that fear The normal response, of course will keep you poor So what's a better response one ample deal flow A well stock pipeline meaning Another one coming down the pike and you know it So that's the position we got to get in and that's the position we'll get in by the end of the day You got to know there's another one coming down the pike You may not necessarily know exactly who they are But you know the pipeline's full of client prospects maturing at their own pace And there'll be another one so that allows you Not to have this fear If so there's clients For all different fee levels And there's clients for example who are not going to pay my fees There's clients who shouldn't Pay my fees and I tell them so It doesn't make good economic sense for you They're doing a one-in-done for example So like I do a lot of work filling seats Campaigns to fill seats for seminars and conferences and a lot of them are one-in-done They're done that year and then they got to reinvent the whole thing Again for next year. Well If the conference is like a two million dollar conference Then it's worth paying me a hundred thousand dollars to do that If it's a two hundred thousand dollar conference It's brain dead stupid to pay me a hundred thousand dollars to do that campaign But I'm not doing the campaign for less than a hundred thousand dollars So charlie you got to find somebody else or you got to do it yourself Let me give you some referrals, but I'm not your guy And you and I'll they get stubborn, you know, no, no, no, no, I want you I'm saying look you're only going to do 250 I'm going to make all the money. You don't want that go here go there, right? So there's clients at every different fee level Okay, and so you can't worry about the one that's not right for you You got to worry about the one it is right for you better positioning so your fee is not debated If if people are trying to negotiate fees with you More often than not if it's not a client selection problem Meaning you're really in front of somebody who should not pay you what it is that you're asking that it is a positioning problem It means that what led up to that conversation wasn't done well, which we're going to spend a lot of time on See you don't So how many in here have been to our tyron? Here's my recommendation Uh go home look at the newspaper under sales help wanted and find like the worst one you can find Um, it'll be a disguised ad but So they'll talk dollars per hour, but they're going to bring you to a cattle call interview So the tip off is when you're coming to a group interview not a one-on-one And they're either going to be the fire alarm guys the back guys the water filter guys Cyclopedia guys are pretty much gone But and if you live in a major city, you may have to go one ring out to find this because it doesn't exist much anymore in In Chicago, but it exists all through Illinois and Indiana So the folks that sell fire alarms in home. They sell vacuum cleaners in the home. They sell water filters in home Go get a job Three months is all it'll take Uh to get from horrible to good if you just determine that you're going to get from horrible to good It will change your life change your life Now we won't make you a better copywriter, but it'll get you over all sorts of stuff That's in your way about getting paid Uh, you just have to you just have to determine that you're going to work through the pain And you're anybody can get good at anybody anybody can learn to sell fire alarm anybody People who stutter People who are ugly People who anybody can learn to sell fire alarm You won't like this understand, right, but it will change your life Multiple income sources So you're not desperate. So we're going to talk about that too if you have income coming in from more and more place It helps with not being worth it not qualified So our common antidotes to that the way most people respond to that if they feel that way Is they're timid they underpriced they negotiate and they back down So better answers one specialized So you can amass sufficient expertise faster So writing copy for anything and everything That will require 10 lifetimes To have great skill So like how many of you know john carl them know the name john carl. Okay, y'all know john, right? So john Now that helbert has left us john pound for pound dollar for dollar Maybe the best copywriter in america for about three things Now he's a good copywriter for a lot of things But he may be the best for about three And he's gotten so good at the three he could probably do it in his sleep Well, it's easier to get there with three than it is with 300 So there's about five things that like I do So I do information marketing stuff And within that the subspecialty is I do seminars conferences conventions. Okay, I do put butts in seats copy And I've gotten great at it I can do health But mostly I do way out alternative health stuff Or I do skincare. I'm real good at skincare Um, so there's like five things There's a lot of stuff. I've not bothered to get good at at all And we'll turn it down Occasionally over the years I've said to somebody I've no I've no I'm no good at that if you want me to get good at it I'll use you as the learning curve, but understand that's what's happening here And it's going to take time and therefore it's going to cost you extra money for me to get good at it because I'm no good at it right now So copywriting skills are not universally applicable There's some principles, but then they are very situational so a quick way To solve your I'm not worth it. I'm not qualified is to Specialized and I'll show you some examples as we go along. I'll show you one right now. So Here is So opportunities I do money making opportunity copy. Great. So here's an ad And don't worry about reading at this I'm not going to look at but here's an ad from 1992 Hey, this is how this is mine How a 29 dollar 95 cent Investment can put you in the most exciting easiest business ever invented or in thousands and tens of thousands of dollars a month Yeah, yeah, yeah Here's an ad from 1998 still running by the way I get a royalty from this I wrote it 1998 Open letter from a once wife broke the brasska housewife who stumbled across a business in beta rich One that you could start to for as little as 15 dollars Now if we took the time And you laid these side by side and you compared them. They're the same damn ad All right Now I had a I had a housewife in one. I had no person for the other one But I mean really they're the same damn ad. Okay. They're six years apart So here's 2007 It's a really good client. I do a lot of work for So help people defend themselves make hundreds thousands even tens of thousands of dollars Month in month out selling stun guns tasers pepper sprays personal arms sitting in camera surveillance systems possibly the drop ship for you Seven reasons to join safety technology Over here. There's 12 reasons. We guarantee you'll fall in love with this business. It's the same damn ad All right 1992 1998 2007 same ad right Is it cheating? No client care. He cares about results. He doesn't care How I'm producing the results Why should he? now Since I get great results. I don't have this not being worth it not qualified there Okay, but if I'd spent my time indiscriminately writing full-page ads for everything and anybody And anybody who was warm had blood in their veins and could write a check. I wouldn't be near as good at this So one answer here To be more qualified and be more worth it is to specialize Even precursor to writing so you can't study everything You can't do it. So you can't pay attention to everything If you were if you're going to get really good at writing opportunity ads You've got to read all the opportunity magazines every month. You've got to answer all the ads You've got to buy everybody's stuff. So you see everything that happens on the back end You got to study the history of marketing opportunities at least back to the 1920s You got to study all the greats who sustained their success in that field over decades You've got to be a real student of that Well, you can't do that if you're also going to be a student of selling Of using full-page ads to sell pots and pans and hair color And eyeglasses and bottled water and every other damn thing. You can't do it. There's not enough hours in a day I mean I speed read I couldn't do it. I'm a better student than almost anybody. I couldn't do it. So you can't do it
